tensions.text: Statista's summed 2024 country-level colocation+scale-colocation total (~7,630MW) exceeds CBRE's Q4 2024 point figure of 4,726MW by ~61% and even exceeds CBRE's own forward-looking '5.8GW by end of 2024' figure by ~32%. Since tamBase's derivation explicitly leans on 'CE-7 = 39.8% of Europe colo MW (Statista 2025)' as a scaling step, and Statista's series is titled 'colocation AND scale colocation' (a broader category than plain colocation), the denominator used to derive the CE-7 share may itself be scope-inflated relative to the CBRE-anchored figures used elsewhere in the chain — echoing the same PDUs+PSUs contamination pattern the node's refinement cycle 1 already flagged and removed for the ceiling.

DC Byte's Poland figure (481MW total capacity in 2024, or 605MW current total supply including pipeline) is more than double Statista's Poland figure for the same year (197MW in 2024), which runs in the opposite direction from the aggregate Statista-vs-CBRE mismatch — suggesting Statista's per-country splits are not uniformly inflated versus other trackers, and the scope/definition gap varies unpredictably by country rather than being a clean single multiplier that could be applied to correct tamBase's CE-7 share.

CBRE's own two 2024 figures diverge: its July 2024 report forecast '5.8GW data centre capacity expected across Europe by end of 2024,' while its Q4 2024 report recorded only 4,726MW of operational colocation supply — a roughly 1.1GW (23%) forecast-vs-actual gap from the same vendor, raising the question of which CBRE figure (if either) is the correct anchor for cross-checking the Statista-derived Europe colo MW base used in tamBase.

absences: No purchasable dataset or country table was found that directly reconciles Statista's colocation+scale-colocation MW definition against CBRE's colocation supply definition on a matched scope — the query's stated goal of settling whether Statista double-counts was not resolved.

Statista's own split between 'standard colocation' and 'scale colocation' shares of European IT power (needed to test whether summing both categories double-counts against CBRE's single 'colocation supply' figure) is paywalled/asterisked in the search results.

No CE-7-specific (as opposed to individual-country or pan-Europe) MW aggregate was found from CBRE or DC Byte that could be directly compared to the 39.8% CE-7 share of Statista's Europe colo MW figure used in tamBase's derivation.

No clarification was found on whether CBRE's 4,726MW 'colocation supply' figure includes hyperscale/scale-colocation leased capacity (the category Statista tracks separately as 'scale colocation'), leaving the fundamental scope-comparability question unanswered.

No 2025 or later CBRE aggregate European colocation MW figure was found to compare against Statista's 2025 country-level sum (~8,676MW), which is the vintage tamBase's derivation cites ('Statista 2025').
